data.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe, overlay)

This overlay applies: the plan defines part of an LLM/agent review system (a REVIEW_RESULT output schema, a per-criterion verdict vocabulary, finding fingerprints, and references to two sibling agents — the orchestrator `ca03` and the Pass-4 coach `75a9`). I tool-grounded the referenced agents/enums/schema and found ONE proven structural gap (the latency/cost metadata field) plus several criteria that affirmatively PASS.

FINDINGS

- [ ] Define the per-pass LATENCY + COST metadata shape the new AC requires — schema location, value vocabulary, units, and per-pass keying (T8: schema/enum field referenced but its value vocabulary never defined).
  - criteria: T8
  - location: Plan §"Acceptance Criteria (added — close G3 coverage)", AC "Capture per-pass LATENCY + COST … in the sidecar metadata"
  - evidence: The AC names a metadata field ("per-pass LATENCY + COST", distinguishing "the fast claim-path check" vs "the out-of-band review") but never defines its shape. The implemented sidecar payload (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:68-82`) and the verdict schema (`src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_verdict.schema.json:46-50`) carry only a free-form `coverage` object (`additionalProperties: true`) describing "what ran" — no latency or cost field. `docs/plan-review-gate.md:183-184` enumerates the captured fields as "per-finding fingerprints + decisions + verification attributes + coverage" — again no latency/cost. An executor cannot tell WHERE these live (a new top-level field? a `coverage.latency`/`coverage.cost` sub-key?), the UNITS (wall-clock ms? tokens? USD?), or how the two passes are KEYED so offline join can separate the fast-path check from the out-of-band review. This is the T8 pattern: a metadata vocabulary referenced by an AC but never specified, so the "refined PASSIVELY from dogfood data" promise has no defined substrate. (criteria_routing.json:336/348 define `latency`/`cost` only as T5a/cost-sizing rubric trigger keys — unrelated to a sidecar metadata schema.)
  - suggested_fix: Add a `metrics` (or `coverage.metrics`) object to the sidecar payload schema with explicit per-pass keys and units, e.g. `{"pass": "det-floor"|"pass1-find"|"pass2-verify"|"pass3-decide"|"pass4-coach"|"claim-path-check", "wall_ms": int, "tokens": {"in": int, "out": int}, "usd": float|null}`, and state that the fast claim-path check and the out-of-band review are recorded as distinct keyed entries so offline analysis can separate them.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk passes under T8)

- No undefined-but-referenced enum: the per-criterion VERDICT vocabulary the sidecar records is fully defined — `verdict` is PASS|BLOCK|INDETERMINATE (`plan_review_verdict.schema.json:10-13`), and the per-finding fields the sidecar slims (`id`, `criteria`, `tier`, `decision`, `severity`, `validity`, `impact`, `priority`, `verification`) are enumerated in `sidecar.py:47-59`. "finding fingerprints" resolves to the content-fingerprint `id` minted by `orchestrator.mint_finding_id` (`orchestrator.py:152-154`).

- Referenced agents exist and are fully specified (tool-verified): the orchestrator `ca03` (dry-frost-zenith, CLOSED) is the SOLE owner that MINTS the finding `id`, and the Pass-4 coach `75a9` (crusty-herb-stand) CONSUMES it via `finding_refs`. The id-ownership protocol is unambiguous and non-circular — `ca03`'s What and `75a9`'s "Caller-visible finding id contract" both state mint-once/consume, matching `orchestrator.py:18` ("SOLE owner") and `sidecar.py` (records the same id). No pink-elephant / instruction-locality antipattern: the sidecar correctly RECORDS the id and does not re-mint it.

- No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement: idempotency placement is explicitly disambiguated — the sidecar is APPEND-ONLY per run (NOT idempotent, by design), while idempotency lives on the ATTESTATION head-state (one current valid signature). This is the kind of state-placement spec T8 looks for, and it is present.

- A fallback for a failed sub-step is specified: the sidecar emit is best-effort and "a failed emit must NEVER fail the review itself" (`sidecar.py:24-37` returns False on any exception) — the observability substrate degrades without taxing the hot path, consistent with the plan's "without taxing rebar's hot paths" intent.

- The rollout/coexistence protocol referenced by the added AC is real and co-located with the schema: `is_unknown_newer_type("REVIEW_RESULT") is False` and `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES = {"SYNC","PRECONDITIONS","REVIEW_RESULT"}` (`reducer/_version.py:44-53`); fsck WARNs on truly-newer types (`_commands/fsck.py:161-170`); the preserve-and-ignore + readers-before-writers rule is documented at `sidecar.py:7-14`. No structural gap here.

Net: one MAJOR-candidate structural gap (undefined latency/cost metadata vocabulary on a referenced schema field); the rest of the T8 surface is clean.
